rulings.Case Title: LARS H ELLNER ET AL VS. PAUL IRACKI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday April 12, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANT PAUL IRACKI, PAULA IRACKI DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurer is sustained without leave to amend to causes of action for fraud (no substantive opposition) and Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress (this cause of action is not available under the facts of this case; additionally damages for mental distress may be recovered per Negligence cause of action, which is separately alleged. See Crisci v. Security Ins. Co. (1967) 66 Cal.2d 425 and Potter v. Firestone (1993) 6 Cal.4th 965). Demurrer is otherwise overruled - cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress is sufficiently alleged; as to the causes of action for wrongful eviction, retaliatory eviction and constructive eviction - allegations are sufficient and complaint alleges that SFRO 37.9(a)(9) was improperly asserted in October 2014, while the premises were offered for rent in July 2015. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PAULINE CHAN ET AL VS. PHILIP S. KWAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday April 12, 2016, line 6. PLAINTIFF PAULINE CHAN, PAUSON KWAN Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration GRANTED. Plaintiff's presented new evidence regarding offers from a third party, not presented and not considered by the Court at the original hearing. Based on the new evidence the Court does not find that the immediate sale, in light of actual and possible higher offers for the property, is more beneficial to all parties. The motion to Compel Immediate Sale is DENIED. = (501/REQ)
